MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil for Bonny Hinners-Krucik (Petitioner).

Mr. O'Neil stated that they have a petition in his Client's hands waiting for her review and signature, and that he will file it. Status on close of estate is continued to January 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. O'Neil that if the petition is not filed, the Administrator will be suspended.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-02-2019 Continued To Nov-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vincent Yu for Petitioner

Mr. Yu stated his office received the Inventory and Appraisal from the referee last week and can file it this week. Mr. Yu further stated the petition for final distribution is almost complete, and things should be ironed out by the end of this month.

The status on close of estate is continued to November 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Wu. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: John Rueppel for Bonny Hinners-Krucik.

The Court stated that before it today is a first and final account by administrator; petition for final distribution filed by the co-personal representatives. Mr. Rueppel stated that once they reached settlement that they discovered that that needed to file a new Inventory & Appraisal (I&A), that there was one property that did not belong in the I&A, that there is also an estate in Georgia, that there were discussions with his client about the phone "tag" with the probate referee, that there was an issue with regard to a coin collection, that he believes that the personal representative has signed the revised I&A, and that they can get going on the petition for distribution. The Court set status on close of the estate for October 2, 2019. The Court stated that a new petition would be filed. The Court ordered the first and final account by administrator, petition for final distribution filed by the co-personal representatives off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Accounting By Billy R. Hinners
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel for Bonnie Hinners-Krucik (Personal Representative).

Mr. Rueppel stated that Billy's accounting is no longer an issue, and requested a 60-day continuance stating that they have to amend the inventory and appraisal, and that he will file a new petition for distribution/accounting. The First and Final Report and Account is continued to August 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of accounting from Billy Hinners is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Removal Of Representative, Suspension Of Powers Pending Hearing, And Appointment Of Neutral
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Continued To Mar-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel for Bonny Hinners-Krucik (Personal Representative).
Richard Franchescini for Billy Hinners.

The Court informed Mr. Franceschini that it needs to receive courtesy copies. Mr. Franceschini stated that they want to be sent out to mediation. Mr. Ruepel stated that he wants to lodge oral opposition in the event the matter does not settle. The Court informed the Parties that if the matter does not settle in mediation, the petition (First and Final Report and Account) will need to be amended. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 20, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 22, 2019. The Petition for Removal of Representative, etc., status on filing of an accounting from Billy Hinners, and the First and Final Report and Account of Co-Personal Representatives are continued to and the Parties are to return on March 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Appearances: Yuliya Magomedov for Petitioner.

Ms. Yuliya stated that they had been trying to remove Mr. Hinner as Co-Administrator, but they were unable to find him, that notice was filed, and that they prepared the accounting, but the Brother was not around to sign it. The Court informed Ms. Magomedov that she will have to file an amended accounting which is to include only the properties in California. The First and Final Report and Account is continued to August 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the Petition for Removal of Co-Personal Representative (subject to review of the order).
